paper_title: Uppateru Estuary Sediment and Foraminiferal Analysis

methods.description: Collection of bottom sediment samples from different sites in the Uppateru estuary to analyze physico-chemical parameters and foraminiferal content.

steps.description: Using the Peterson grab, 14 bottom sediment samples from different sites in the Uppateru estuary were collected.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples using Peterson grab

steps.description: The location of each sample site was determined by two permanent landmarks.
steps.searchable_summary: determine sample site locations using landmarks

steps.description: The upper 2 cm of sediment collected was sampled and stored in polyethylene bottles to avoid contamination.
steps.searchable_summary: store sediment samples in polyethylene bottles

steps.description: Certain physico-chemical parameters like salinity, pH, Temp, Ec, Do, TSM etc., were measured using Elico water analysis kit.
steps.searchable_summary: measure physico-chemical parameters using Elico kit

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing foraminiferal content in sediment samples to assess marine pollution.

steps.description: Approximately 10 g of sediment sample were first kept in the oven for drying overnight at 60˚C temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: dry sediment samples in oven at 60˚C

steps.description: The dried samples were then soaked in water in a tray.
steps.searchable_summary: soak dried sediment samples in water

steps.description: To disperse the lumps of clay in the samples, sodium hexa-meta-phosphate was added and kept for a day.
steps.searchable_summary: add sodium hexa-meta-phosphate to disperse clay

steps.description: Then hydrogen peroxide was added and kept overnight to disintegrate organic matter.
steps.searchable_summary: add hydrogen peroxide to disintegrate organic matter

steps.description: Finally, the samples were washed through a 63 µ sieve with a slow shower with low water pressure to prevent the foraminiferal test breakage.
steps.searchable_summary: wash samples through 63 µ sieve with low water pressure

steps.description: The sand fraction thus obtained was dried and examined for foraminifera.
steps.searchable_summary: examine dried sand fraction for foraminifera

steps.description: The total foraminiferal number (TFN) in each sample was completed and standardized to 10 gram dry sediment.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate and standardize total foraminiferal number

steps.description: The Total Species Number (TSN), is the absolute value of diversity in the study area.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate total species number for diversity
